notes: RESOLVED: Q4's control ties into the Q11 circuit = the FRONT-IR CARRIER node. Q11 is driven by STM32 PB10 / TIM2 (the 1 kHz FRONT_IR_Q11_PULSE). So the bottom cliff IR emitters are modulated by the SAME 1 kHz lock-in carrier as the front IR panel; STM32 control = PB10 (shared, no separate cliff enable). Implication: the cliff SENSE lines (e.g. right = PA3 analog) can be synchronously demodulated against PB10, exactly like the front panel demod

RESOLVED: J17:5 + GND = the side IR TRANSCEIVER's RECEIVER output. User clarified the 'unique side sensor' is a transceiver (priyomoperedatchik = emit+receive), which he had mis-called a 'receiver'. Its EMITTER is the FORWARD-facing LED, wired in SERIES with the cliff down-LED on the shared J17:2 drive (2 LEDs in series: one faces DOWN=cliff, one faces FORWARD=side); emitter-string anode = 5V via D3 -> J17:8. So pin5 = side-transceiver receiver out; emitter drive = pin2; this is SEPARATE from the 3-pin base/dock receiver on pins 6/8
